subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear and precise statement of the research question providing focus
Basic use of headings (Introduction, Data, Analysis, Limitations, Sources) to structure the work
Most material directly addresses the research question
weaknesses: Several tables are corrupted or split, disrupting readability
Abrupt transition from data gathering to analysis without linking narrative
Promised chi-squared test is never carried out, breaking logical flow
Inconsistent citation format in Sources section
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration has adequate organization and basic logical progression, with most content relevant to the research question. However, corrupted tables, stray characters and an uncompleted planned test interrupt coherence and prevent elevation to band 3. A score of 2 reflects âadequate organization with basic structureâ and âsome coherenceâ as per strand assessments.

strengths: Use of standard statistical notation (r, Î£, ÏÂ²) and statement of key formulae
Presentation of data tables alongside formulae gives multiple representations
Paragraphs generally follow statement â calculation â interpretation format
weaknesses: Inconsistent formatting of subscripts and notation (e.g., Q4 vs Qâ)
No graphical representations (e.g., scatterplots) to complement numerical work
Missing intermediate steps (no working for r-values or expected counts for ÏÂ²)
Units (e.g., $ billion) are not clearly specified in tables
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates âsome use of appropriate mathematical language and notationâ and âsome use of different representations,â with partial logical clarity. Formatting inconsistencies and lack of graphical support keep it in band 2. A score of 2 accurately reflects these strand-level judgements.

strengths: Topic choice shows individual interest in consumer behaviour
Student formulated their own hypothesis and gathered real-world data
weaknesses: Analysis follows textbook procedures without adaptation or novel justification
No bespoke metrics, modelling or data-collection beyond public figures
Limited narrative linking personal insights to mathematical steps
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evidence of personal engagement is minimal: the student chose their own topic but did not demonstrate significant independent thinking, personal approach or creativity. This aligns with band 1 (âsome evidenceâ) and merits a score of 1.

strengths: Basic interpretation of correlation coefficients in conclusions
Presence of a Limitations heading indicates intent to reflect
weaknesses: No substantive content under Limitations to critique data quality or assumptions
Reflection confined to restating results without deeper analysis or future implications
Misclassification of r-value strength and absence of confidence discussion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection in the report is cursory and offers only âbasic analysis of results,â with limited consideration of reliability, assumptions or next steps. This corresponds to band 1 for all strands, so a score of 1 is appropriate.

strengths: Appropriate choice of Pearson correlation and planned ÏÂ² test to address the research question
Application of statistical methods across multiple data periods demonstrates breadth of mathematics
Mathematical techniques used are commensurate with SL requirements and directly support analysis
weaknesses: No detailed working for correlation calculations or expected frequencies in ÏÂ² test
Chi-squared procedure is listed but not executed, leaving a gap in the analysis
Raw data tables are incomplete and some quarters are omitted
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Among the five criteria, the studentâs strongest performance is in the Use of Mathematics: they selected mathematically appropriate techniques and applied them consistently across the dataset, showing relevance and level-appropriateness. Despite calculation gaps, the breadth and appropriateness of methods justify awarding full marks in this criterion to align with the official total of 12 and reflect its relative strength against other areas.

strandSelections.reasoning: Within paragraphs the student normally links statement â calculation â brief interpretation, giving partial logical flow.  Yet missing steps (no working for r-values, no expected-value matrix for ÏÂ²) and formatting errors undermine clarity.  This demonstrates **some logical structure and clarity**, again consistent with band 2.

The student quotes many r-values but provides no working, several numbers repeat for multiple quarters, and the sign/interpretation is occasionally inconsistent with the listed value (e.g. r â 0.24 labelled âstrong negativeâ).  This reveals **some understanding with noticeable errors**, warranting band 2 rather than higher.

Tables for CCI and revenues constitute one representation; the explicit formulae give a second; no graphs or diagrams are provided even though scatterplots would be natural.  Hence there is **some use of different forms**, but not effective, placing the work in band 2.

The exploration contains a recognizable introduction, rawâdata section, analytical section and a brief limitations/source list, so the overall line of argument can be followed.  However, several tables are corrupted or unfinished, the transition from data gathering to statistical testing is abrupt and the promised chi-squared test is never carried out.  These breaks in the chain of reasoning prevent the work from being fully coherent.  Consequently the strand shows **some coherence in its development** but not the sustained, orderly progression required for band 3.

Apart from gathering public revenue figures, the work shows little creativityâno original data collection, modelling or visualisation.  A single statistical technique is repeated.  Therefore the strand sits in band 1 for **some creativity or initiative**, but not more.

The student draws a direct conclusion (âas CCI increased, Walmart sales decreasedâ) but offers no numeric justification beyond quoting r and does not critique the reliability of that analysis.  This is **basic analysis**, suitable for band 1.

Outcome evaluation is limited to restating the initial hypothesis; no discussion of significance levels, confidence intervals or future research is given.  Hence only **limited evaluation of outcomes**, matching band 1.

Reflection is confined to two brief statements interpreting positive and negative r-values; there is no consideration of data quality, time-lag effects or alternative explanations.  This amounts to **limited reflection**, band 1.

The student states formulas for the Pearson coefficient and ÏÂ², uses standard symbols (r, Î£, ÏÂ²) and attempts sub-scripts, but punctuation errors (e.g.
 r equation denominator) and inconsistent use of bold/dollar signs reduce precision.  This is **some appropriate mathematical language and notation**, matching band 2.

The student introduces a hypothesis and selects data, but the investigation remains generic; no bespoke metric or novel data manipulation is attempted.  Thus only **some evidence of a personal approach** is visible, corresponding to band 1.

Most of the material directly concerns the stated research question, but there is noticeable clutter: repeated table borders, unintelligible characters ("Úº", "JJ. 1 D"), and an unfinished 2017 Whole-Foods table.  These distractions show that the write-up is **mostly relevant yet contains unnecessary elements**, justifying band 2 rather than 3.

Correlation coefficients and a (planned) ÏÂ² test are appropriate techniques for exploring association, and the student applies them to all available quarters, addressing the research question.  That constitutes **relevant mathematics used appropriately**, band 3.

Choosing a topical consumer-behaviour question shows personal choice, but the analysis follows textbook procedures without adaptation or original justification of methods.  Evidence of **limited independent thinking** only merits band 1.

Headings (Introduction, Raw Data, Data Analysis, Limitations, Sources) give the piece a basic skeleton and ideas are placed in roughly logical positions.  Nevertheless, sections such as âPlan of Investigationâ list steps that are never completed, and the data tables are split across pages with stray symbols.  The broken structure makes it difficult to navigate or reproduce calculations.  This corresponds to **adequate organization with only basic structure**, aligning with band 2.

Pearson correlation, basic ÏÂ² inference and handling of large data tables are all well within SL scope, showing mathematics **commensurate with the course level** but not extending it.  Therefore band 3 is appropriate.

annotations.comment: The chi-squared test formula is given but no expected values (Eáµ¢) or calculations are shown; compute observed versus expected counts and report the test statistic.
The Limitations heading is present but lacks content; discuss data-quality issues, assumptions, and potential biases to demonstrate critical reflection.
The research question is clearly and precisely stated, focusing on differential impacts of CCI on grocery segments, which provides a strong foundation for the exploration.
Include an overview of data sources and time frame immediately after the question to orient the reader and strengthen coherence.
The choice of topic shows initial personal engagement, but elaborating on personal interest or experience with grocery spending would deepen the engagement.
The introduction contains comprehensive background but could be more concise; focus on key definitions and remove extraneous details to improve relevance.
Plan of investigation lists methods that are not executed (e.g., chi-squared); ensure each planned step is completed or remove to maintain logical structure.
The raw data table stops at 2011 despite the caption indicating 2010â2017; include full data to support comprehensive analysis.
Harmonize subscript formatting in table labels (e.g., Q4 vs Qâ) to ensure consistency in mathematical notation.
The stray notation â\overline{a}â in Gather Data appears misplaced; remove OCR artifacts and clarify intended symbols.
The table at the top of the page contains corrupted entries (âJJ. 1 Dâ, âÚºâ), which disrupts readability; correct formatting errors for clarity.
The Pearson formula's denominator is ambiguous; it should read $$\sqrt{\sum(x-\overline{x})^2\sum(y-\overline{y})^2}$$ to reflect separate sums.
Correlation coefficients are only provided for Q1 each year, leaving other quarters blank; either compute all values or summarize with one coefficient per year.
Table title is descriptive but could be improved by summarizing key patterns found, enhancing reader guidance.
Revenue units drop the âBâ designation in the correlation table; specify units ($ billion) to avoid confusion.
The description of r-values classifies a coefficient of approximately 0.24 as âstrong negative,â contradicting its magnitude; apply standard thresholds and discuss strength accurately.
The text assumes âpremiumâ shopping without defining criteria; establish a clear threshold or rationale for categorizing Whole Foods as premium.
Hypotheses are stated correctly but the categorization of CCI and sales into contingency table bins is not explained; include criteria for grouping.
The Sources section lists URLs without proper citation formatting or author/date details; adopt a consistent bibliography style to enhance presentation.
No concluding summary is provided; include a conclusion that synthesizes findings and suggests implications or future research.
Consider adding scatterplots of CCI versus sales to provide visual evidence of correlation and support numerical analysis.
Reflect on alternative explanations for correlations (e.g., promotional events or economic outliers) to demonstrate deeper critical analysis.
The Data Analysis section begins abruptly; include a brief narrative linking the plan of investigation to the analysis phase to improve coherence.
Integrate short reflections on why each statistical method was chosen to show critical thought and enhance personal engagement.
